patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/547

This is backlog tracking only in the current immutable-history/read implementation lane. Reuse the public output contracts and avoid package-specific sentinels.
GH-547: SDK exporters and renderers cannot suppress host rendering of handled output

Track the SDK contract gap where a package-owned exporter or renderer cannot explicitly mark a handled result as already emitted, causing host rendering to corrupt stdout or forcing stderr workarounds.

A typed, documented suppression contract is shared by exporter, renderer, service override, CLI, and MCP dispatch; default behavior remains backward compatible; runtime and package-harness tests cover handled output, suppressed output, JSON/TOON, errors, and telemetry; a packed temp package proves stdout remains uncorrupted

Implement a package exporter or renderer that streams or writes its own output, return a normal handled result, and observe the host render the result again because no public suppression sentinel exists.

Public typed SDK contracts let handled exporter, renderer, and output-service paths explicitly suppress duplicate host rendering while preserving structured results, telemetry, and JSON/TOON contract clarity.
Package authors must corrupt stdout with duplicate output, misuse stderr, or return shapes that the host still renders.
SDK exporter, renderer, and output service contracts
Package authors cannot safely implement streaming, binary, or already-rendered export workflows through public SDK primitives.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-14: all-status searches for GH-547, suppress host rendering, exporter renderer suppression, and stdout corruption found no existing item. GitHub issue #547 is canonical external evidence; this item is deliberately left open and unclaimed.
